title: Judges 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 After Joshua died, the Israelites asked the LORD, "Who will go up first to fight the Canaanites for us?"
2 The LORD answered, "Judah will go up. I have given the land into their hands."

3 Then the men of Judah said to their brothers, the Simeonites, "Come up with us to the land given to us. Let us fight the Canaanites together. Then we will go with you to your land." So the Simeonites went with them.
4 When Judah attacked, the LORD gave the Canaanites and Perizzites into their hands. They killed ten thousand men at Bezek.
5 There they found Adoni-bezek and fought him. They killed the Canaanites and Perizzites.
6 Adoni-bezek ran away, but they chased him. They caught him and cut off his thumbs and big toes.
7 Then Adoni-bezek said, "Seventy kings with their thumbs and big toes cut off picked up scraps under my table. As I did to them, God has paid me back." They took him to Jerusalem, and he died there.
8 Then the men of Judah fought against Jerusalem and took it. They killed the people of the city and set it on fire.
9 After that, the men of Judah marched down to fight the Canaanites. These Canaanites lived in the hill country, in the Negev, and in the low hills.
10 Judah also marched against the Canaanites in Hebron, also called Kiriath-arba. They killed Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.
11 From there they marched against the people of Debir, also called Kiriath-sepher.
12 Caleb said, "I will give my daughter Acsah as a wife to the man who attacks Kiriath-sepher and takes it."
13 So Othniel, the son of Caleb's younger brother Kenaz, took the city. Caleb gave him his daughter Acsah as a wife.
14 One day Acsah came to Othniel. She urged him to ask her father for a field. When she got off her donkey, Caleb asked her, "What do you want?"
15 She answered, "Give me a gift. You have given me land in the Negev. Now give me springs of water too." So Caleb gave her the upper and the lower springs.
16 The family of Moses' father-in-law, the Kenite, went up with the men of Judah. They went from the City of Palms to the Wilderness of Judah near Arad. They went to live among the people there.
17 Then the men of Judah went with their brothers, the Simeonites. They attacked the Canaanites in Zephath and fully destroyed the city. So it was called Hormah.
18 Judah also took Gaza, Ashkelon, and Ekron, each with its land.
19 The LORD was with Judah. They took the hill country for their own. But they could not push out the people of the plains, because those people had iron chariots.
20 Just as Moses had promised, Judah gave Hebron to Caleb. Caleb pushed out the three sons of Anak from there.
21 But the Benjamites did not push out the Jebusites in Jerusalem. So the Jebusites still live there among the Benjamites even today.
22 The family of Joseph also attacked Bethel, and the LORD was with them.
23 They sent spies to Bethel, also called Luz.
24 The spies saw a man coming out of the city. They said to him, "Please show us the way into the city. Then we will be kind to you."
25 So the man showed them the way in. They killed the people of the city, but they let that man and his family go.
26 The man went to the land of the Hittites. He built a city and named it Luz. That is still its name today.
27 At that time Manasseh did not push out the people of Beth-shean, Taanach, Dor, Ibleam, Megiddo, or their villages. The Canaanites wanted to stay in that land.
28 When Israel grew stronger, they made the Canaanites work for them. But they never pushed them all out.
29 Ephraim also did not push out the Canaanites in Gezer. So the Canaanites kept living among them in Gezer.
30 Zebulun did not push out the people of Kitron and Nahalol. So the Canaanites lived among them and became forced workers.
31 Asher did not push out the people of Acco, Sidon, Ahlab, Achzib, Helbah, Aphik, and Rehob.
32 So the Asherites lived among the Canaanites in the land, because they did not push them out.

33 Naphtali did not push out the people of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath. So the Naphtalites also lived among the Canaanites in the land. But the people of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath became forced workers for them.
34 The Amorites forced the Danites into the hill country. They did not let them come down to the plain.
35 The Amorites wanted to stay in Mount Heres, Aijalon, and Shaalbim. But when the family of Joseph grew stronger, they made the Amorites work for them.
36 The border of the Amorites went from the Pass of Akrabbim to Sela and farther.

title: Judges 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 The angel of the LORD went up from Gilgal to Bochim. He said, "I brought you out of Egypt. I led you into the land that I promised to your fathers. I said, 'I will never break my covenant with you.
2 You must not make a covenant with the people of this land. You must break down their altars.' But you did not obey my voice. What is this you have done?
3 So now I tell you: I will not drive out these people before you. They will be like sharp thorns in your sides. Their gods will be a trap for you."
4 When the angel of the LORD spoke these words to all Israel, the people cried out loud and wept.
5 So they called that place Bochim. They gave offerings there to the LORD.
6 After Joshua sent the people away, the Israelites went out to take the land as their own. Each one went to his family land.
7 The people served the LORD all the days of Joshua. They also served him in the days of the elders who lived longer than Joshua. These elders had seen all the great works the LORD did for Israel.
8 Joshua son of Nun, the servant of the LORD, died at the age of 110.
9 They buried him in his family land, at Timnath-heres. It was in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
10 After that whole generation died like their fathers, another generation rose up. They did not know the LORD or the works that he had done for Israel.
11 The Israelites did evil things that the LORD saw. They worshiped the Baals (false gods).

12 So they left the LORD, the God of their fathers. He had brought them out of the land of Egypt. But they followed other gods of the peoples around them. They bowed down to them and made the LORD angry.
13 They left him and served Baal and the Ashtoreths (false goddesses).
14 Then the LORD burned with anger against Israel. He handed them over to robbers who took their things. He sold them to their enemies all around. They could not stand against them anymore.
15 Wherever Israel went out to fight, the hand of the LORD was against them. He brought trouble, just as he had promised. So they were very upset.
16 Then the LORD chose leaders called judges. The judges saved them from the robbers.

17 But Israel did not listen to their judges. Instead, they were unfaithful with other gods and bowed down to them. They quickly turned away from the way of their fathers. Their fathers had obeyed the LORD's commands. But these people did not follow their example.

18 Whenever the LORD chose a judge for Israel, the LORD was with that judge. He saved them from their enemies while the judge was still alive. The LORD felt sorry for them when they cried under those who hurt them.
19 But when the judge died, the people became more wicked than their fathers. They followed other gods to serve and bow down to them. They would not give up their evil ways or their stubborn hearts.
20 So the LORD burned with anger against Israel. He said, "This nation has broken the covenant I gave to their fathers. They have not listened to my voice.
21 So I will no longer drive out the nations that Joshua left when he died.
22 In this way I will test Israel. I will see if they will keep the way of the LORD as their fathers did."
23 That is why the LORD left those nations in the land. He did not drive them out right away. He did not hand them over to Joshua.

title: Judges 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 These are the nations that the LORD left in the land. He used them to test the Israelites who had not fought in Canaan.
2 He did this to teach war to the new young men of Israel who had not learned it before.
3 They were the five rulers of the Philistines, all the Canaanites, the Sidonians, and the Hivites. The Hivites lived in the mountains of Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon to Lebo-hamath.
4 These nations were left to test Israel. The LORD wanted to see if they would obey his commands, which he gave their fathers through Moses.
5 So the Israelites lived among the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
6 They married their daughters. They gave their own daughters to their sons. And they served their gods.
7 The Israelites did evil before the LORD. They forgot the LORD their God. They served the Baals and the Asherahs.
8 Then the LORD became very angry with Israel. He gave them to Cushan-rishathaim, the king of Aram-naharaim. They served him for eight years.
9 But the Israelites cried out to the LORD. So he sent a rescuer to save them. His name was Othniel, son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.
10 The Spirit of the LORD came on him. He became Israel's judge and went out to war. The LORD gave Cushan-rishathaim, king of Aram, into his hand. Othniel won the fight.
11 So the land had peace for forty years. Then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
12 Again the Israelites did evil before the LORD. So the LORD gave Eglon king of Moab power over Israel, because of their evil.
13 Eglon joined with the Ammonites and Amalekites. He attacked and defeated Israel. He took the City of Palms.
14 The Israelites served Eglon king of Moab for eighteen years.

15 Again the Israelites cried out to the LORD. So he sent a rescuer named Ehud. Ehud was the son of Gera, from the tribe of Benjamin. He was left-handed. They sent him with a gift to Eglon king of Moab.
16 Ehud made a short sword sharp on both sides. He tied it to his right leg under his coat.
17 He brought the gift to Eglon king of Moab. Eglon was a very fat man.
18 After Ehud gave the gift, he sent away the men who carried it.
19 But near the idols at Gilgal, he turned back. He said, "O king, I have a secret message for you." The king said, "Silence!" Then all his servants went out.
20 Eglon was sitting alone in his cool upstairs room. Ehud came near and said, "I have a word from God for you." So the king stood up from his seat.
21 Then Ehud reached out with his left hand. He took the sword from his right leg and pushed it into Eglon's stomach.
22 Even the handle went in after the blade. The fat closed over the sword. Ehud could not pull it out. And the waste came out.
23 Then Ehud went out through the back porch. He shut the doors of the upstairs room and locked them.
24 After Ehud left, the servants came back. They found the doors of the upstairs room locked. They said, "He must be using the toilet in the cool room."
25 So they waited and waited. They began to worry. The doors were still shut. Then they took the key and opened them. There was their lord, dead on the floor.
26 While the servants waited, Ehud escaped. He passed the idols and got away to Seirah.
27 When he came to Seirah, he blew a horn in the hills of Ephraim. The Israelites came down from the hills with him. He became their leader.

28 He said to them, "Follow me! The LORD has given your enemies the Moabites into your hand." So they followed him down. They took the shallow crossings of the Jordan River. They did not let anyone cross over.
29 At that time they killed about ten thousand Moabites. All were strong and brave men. Not one of them escaped.
30 That day Moab lost to Israel. And the land had peace for eighty years.
31 After Ehud came Shamgar son of Anath. He also saved Israel. He struck down six hundred Philistines with a long farm stick.

title: Judges 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 After Ehud died, the Israelites again did evil things before the LORD.
2 So the LORD gave them over to Jabin, king of Canaan, who ruled in Hazor. The leader of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-hagoyim.
3 Then the Israelites cried out to the LORD. Jabin had nine hundred iron war-chariots. He had treated the Israelites cruelly for twenty years.
4 At that time, Deborah was leading Israel as judge. She was a woman who spoke for God. She was the wife of Lappidoth.
5 She would sit under the Palm of Deborah, between Ramah and Bethel in the hill country of Ephraim. The Israelites would go up to her for judgment.

6 She called for Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali. She said to him, "The LORD, the God of Israel, is commanding you: 'Go and go up to Mount Tabor. Take with you ten thousand men from Naphtali and Zebulun.
7 I will bring Sisera, the leader of Jabin's army, out to the River Kishon. I will bring his chariots and his troops there. And I will give him into your hand.'"
8 Barak said to her, "If you will go with me, I will go. But if you will not go with me, I will not go."

9 "I will surely go with you," Deborah replied. "But the road you are taking will not bring you honor. The LORD will give Sisera into the hand of a woman." So Deborah got up and went with Barak to Kedesh.
10 There Barak called for Zebulun and Naphtali. Ten thousand men followed him, and Deborah also went with him.
11 Now Heber the Kenite had moved away from the other Kenites. The Kenites came from Hobab, the father-in-law of Moses. Heber set up his tent by the great tree of Zaanannim, near Kedesh.
12 People told Sisera that Barak son of Abinoam had gone up Mount Tabor.
13 So Sisera called for all his nine hundred iron chariots. He called for all the men with him, from Harosheth-hagoyim to the River Kishon.

14 Then Deborah said to Barak, "Get up! This is the day the LORD has given Sisera into your hand. The LORD has gone ahead of you!" So Barak came down from Mount Tabor with ten thousand men following him.
15 And the LORD defeated Sisera with the sword. He defeated all his chariot drivers and all his army in front of Barak. Sisera left his chariot and ran away on foot.
16 Then Barak chased the chariots and the army as far as Harosheth-hagoyim. The whole army of Sisera died by the sword. Not one man was left.
17 Meanwhile, Sisera ran on foot to the tent of Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite. There was peace between Jabin king of Hazor and the family of Heber the Kenite.
18 Jael went out to greet Sisera. She said to him, "Come in, my lord. Come in with me. Do not be afraid." So he went into her tent, and she covered him with a blanket.
19 Sisera said to her, "Please give me a little water to drink. I am thirsty." So she opened a container of milk. She gave him a drink and covered him again.
20 "Stand at the door of the tent," he said. "If anyone comes and asks, 'Is there a man here?' say, 'No.'"

21 But Sisera was very tired and fell asleep. Then Jael, Heber's wife, took a tent peg. She picked up a hammer and went quietly to Sisera. She drove the peg through the side of his head into the ground, and he died.

22 When Barak came chasing after Sisera, Jael went out to greet him. She said to him, "Come, and I will show you the man you are looking for." So he went in with her. And there lay Sisera, dead, with a tent peg through the side of his head.
23 On that day God defeated Jabin, king of Canaan, before the Israelites.
24 The hand of the Israelites grew stronger and stronger against Jabin, king of Canaan. At last they destroyed him.

title: Judges 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 On that day, Deborah and Barak son of Abinoam sang this song:
2 "When the leaders lead the way in Israel, when the people offer themselves freely, bless the LORD.
3 Listen, O kings! Listen carefully, O princes! I will sing to the LORD. I will sing praise to the LORD, the God of Israel.
4 O LORD, when You went out from Seir, when You marched from the land of Edom, the earth shook. The skies poured out rain. The clouds poured down water.
5 The mountains shook before the LORD, the One of Sinai, before the LORD, the God of Israel.
6 In the days of Shamgar son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the main roads were empty. Travelers took the side paths.
7 Village life stopped. It ended in Israel, until I, Deborah, arose — a mother in Israel.
8 When they chose new gods, then war came to their gates. Not one shield or spear was found among forty thousand in Israel.
9 My heart is with the leaders of Israel, with those who gave themselves freely. Bless the LORD!
10 You who ride white donkeys, you who sit on fine saddle cloths, you who walk the road — think about this.
11 Listen to the singers at the water wells. There they tell of the right deeds of the LORD, the right deeds of His village people in Israel. Then the people of the LORD went down to the gates.
12 'Wake up, wake up, O Deborah! Wake up, wake up, sing a song! Arise, O Barak, and take your captives, O son of Abinoam!'
13 Then those who were left came down to the great men. The people of the LORD came down to me against the mighty.
14 Some came from Ephraim, with their roots in Amalek. Benjamin came with your people after you. The commanders came down from Machir. Those who carry the leader's staff came from Zebulun.
15 The leaders of Issachar were with Deborah. Issachar was with Barak, rushing into the valley at his heels. In the clans of Reuben, there was a hard time deciding.
16 Why did you sit among the sheep pens to hear the whistling for the flocks? In the clans of Reuben, there was a hard time deciding.
17 Gilead stayed across the Jordan River. Dan, why did you wait around by the ships? Asher stayed at the coast and remained in his harbors.
18 Zebulun was a people who put their lives in danger. Naphtali, too, on the high places of battle.
19 Kings came and fought. Then the kings of Canaan fought at Taanach by the waters of Megiddo. But they took no silver as stolen goods.
20 From the skies the stars fought. From their paths in the sky, they fought against Sisera.
21 The River Kishon washed them away — the ancient river, the River Kishon. March on, O my soul, in strength!
22 Then the horse hooves made a loud sound. His strong horses ran wildly.
23 'Curse Meroz,' says the angel of the LORD. 'Curse her people with strong words. For they did not come to help the LORD, to help the LORD against the mighty.'
24 Most blessed among women is Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite. Most blessed among the women who live in tents.
25 He asked for water, and she gave him milk. In a fine bowl she brought him thick milk.
26 She reached for the tent pin, her right hand for the worker's hammer. She struck Sisera and crushed his head. She broke and went through the side of his head.
27 At her feet he fell down. He fell. There he lay still. At her feet he fell down. He fell. Where he fell down, there he fell dead.
28 Sisera's mother looked through the window. She looked through the window screen and cried out sadly: 'Why is his chariot so slow in coming? Why are his chariot wheels so late?'
29 Her wisest ladies answer. Indeed, she keeps telling herself,
30 'Are they not finding and dividing the stolen goods? A girl or two for each warrior, dyed clothes for Sisera, clothes with fine designs for the neck of the one who took them?'
31 So may all Your enemies die, O LORD! But may those who love You shine like the sun when it is most bright." And the land had rest for forty years.

title: Judges 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Again the Israelites did evil things that the LORD could see. So He gave them into the power of Midian for seven years.
2 Midian was stronger than Israel. Because of the Midianites, the Israelites made hiding places. They hid in the mountains, in caves, and in safe places.
3 Whenever the Israelites planted their crops, the Midianites came up and attacked them. The Amalekites and other people from the east came too.
4 They set up camp against Israel as far as Gaza. They destroyed the crops that grew on the land. They left Israel with no food, no sheep, no oxen, and no donkeys.
5 The Midianites came with their animals and tents like a big cloud of locusts. They and their camels were too many to count. They came into the land to destroy it.
6 Midian made Israel very poor. So the Israelites called out to the LORD for help.
7 When the Israelites called out to the LORD because of Midian,
8 He sent them a prophet. The prophet said, "This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says. I brought you up out of Egypt. I brought you out of the place where you were slaves.
9 I saved you from the power of Egypt. I saved you from all the people who hurt you. I drove them out and gave you their land.
10 I said to you, 'I am the LORD your God. Do not be afraid of the gods of the Amorites. You live in their land.' But you did not listen to me."

11 Then the angel of the LORD came to Ophrah. He sat down under the oak tree. The tree belonged to Joash the Abiezrite. His son Gideon was beating out wheat in a winepress. He did this to hide it from the Midianites.
12 The angel of the LORD appeared to Gideon. He said, "**The LORD is with you, mighty warrior**."

13 Gideon replied, "Please, my Lord. If the LORD is with us, why has all this happened to us? Where are all His wonders that our fathers told us about? They said, 'Did the LORD not bring us out of Egypt?' But now the LORD has left us. He has given us into Midian's power."
14 The LORD turned to him and said, "Go in the strength you have. Save Israel from the power of Midian. Am I not sending you?"
15 Gideon replied, "Please, my Lord, how can I save Israel? My family is the weakest in Manasseh. I am the youngest in my father's house."
16 The LORD replied, "Surely I will be with you. You will strike down all the Midianites as one man."
17 Gideon answered, "If You are kind to me, give me a sign. Show me that it is You speaking with me.
18 Please do not go away from this place until I return. Let me bring my offering and set it before You." The LORD said, "I will stay here until you return."

19 So Gideon went in and prepared a young goat. He also made bread without yeast from a large amount of flour. He put the meat in a basket and the soup in a pot. He brought them out under the oak tree.
20 The angel of God said to him, "Take the meat and the bread. Lay them on this rock. Pour out the soup." And Gideon did so.
21 Then the angel of the LORD held out the tip of his staff. He touched the meat and the bread. Fire came up from the rock and burned them up. Then the angel of the LORD was gone from his sight.
22 Gideon saw that it was the angel of the LORD. He said, "Oh no, Lord GOD! I have seen the angel of the LORD face to face!"
23 But the LORD said to him, "Peace be with you. Do not be afraid. You will not die."
24 So Gideon built an altar to the LORD there. He called it The LORD Is Peace. To this day it stands in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.
25 That very night the LORD said to Gideon, "Take your father's young bull. Also take a second bull seven years old. Tear down your father's altar to Baal. Cut down the Asherah pole beside it.
26 Then build a proper altar to the LORD your God on this safe place. Use the wood of the Asherah pole you cut down. Take the second bull and offer it as a burnt offering."
27 So Gideon took ten of his servants and did as the LORD told him. But he was afraid of his father's family and the men of the city. So he did it at night instead of in the day.
28 The men of the city got up in the morning. They saw Baal's altar torn down. The Asherah pole was cut down beside it. The second bull was offered on the new altar.
29 They said to one another, "Who did this?" They asked many questions. Then they were told, "Gideon son of Joash did it."
30 Then the men of the city said to Joash, "Bring out your son. He must die. He has torn down Baal's altar and cut down the Asherah pole beside it."

31 But Joash said to all who stood against him, "Are you fighting for Baal? Are you trying to save him? Anyone who speaks for him will be put to death by morning! If Baal is a god, let him fight for himself. His altar has been torn down."
32 So on that day Gideon was called Jerubbaal. This means "Let Baal fight with him," because he had torn down Baal's altar.
33 Then all the Midianites, Amalekites, and other people from the east came together. They crossed the Jordan and camped in the Valley of Jezreel.
34 So the Spirit of the LORD came on Gideon. He blew the ram's horn and called the Abiezrites to follow him.
35 Gideon sent messengers all through Manasseh, and also to Asher, Zebulun, and Naphtali. He called them to fight. So they came up to meet him.
36 Then Gideon said to God, "You promised to save Israel by my hand.
37 look, I will place a piece of wool on the threshing floor. Let the wool be wet with dew. Let all the ground be dry. Then I will know You will save Israel by my hand."
38 And that is what happened. When Gideon got up the next morning, he squeezed the wool. He wrung out the dew — a bowl full of water.
39 Then Gideon said to God, "Do not be angry with me. Let me speak one more time. Please let me test the wool one more time. This time let the wool be dry, and let the ground be covered with dew."
40 That night God did so. Only the wool was dry, and dew covered the ground.

title: Judges 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Early in the morning, Jerubbaal (that is, Gideon) and all his men camped by the spring of Harod. The camp of Midian was north of them in the valley near the hill of Moreh.
2 Then the LORD said to Gideon, "You have too many men. I cannot give Midian into your hands like this. Israel might boast against me and say, 'My own hand has saved me.'
3 So tell the people out loud: 'Whoever is afraid and shaking may go home and leave Mount Gilead.'" So 22,000 men turned back, but 10,000 stayed.

4 Then the LORD said to Gideon, "There are still too many men. Take them down to the water. I will test them for you there. If I say, 'This one will go with you,' he will go. If I say, 'This one will not go,' he will not go."

5 So Gideon brought the men down to the water. The LORD said to him, "Set apart those who lap the water with their tongues like a dog. Keep them apart from those who get down on their knees to drink."
6 The number of men who lapped the water with their hands to their mouths was 300. All the others got down on their knees to drink.
7 Then the LORD said to Gideon, "With these 300 men who lapped the water I will save you. I will give the Midianites into your hand. Send all the others home."
8 So Gideon sent the rest of the Israelites to their tents. He kept the 300 men. They took the food and the rams' horns of the others. The camp of Midian lay below him in the valley.
9 That night the LORD said to Gideon, "Get up and go down against the camp. I have given it into your hand.
10 But if you are afraid to go, take your servant Purah with you.
11 Listen to what they are saying. Then you will become brave to attack the camp." So he went with his servant Purah to the edge of the camp where the guards stood.
12 The Midianites, the Amalekites, and all the other people of the east had filled the valley. They were like a huge swarm of locusts. Their camels were as many as the sand on the shore.

13 When Gideon arrived, a man was telling his friend about a dream. "Look, I had a dream," he said. "I saw a loaf of barley bread come rolling down into the Midianite camp. It hit the tent so hard that the tent fell over and collapsed."
14 His friend replied, "This is nothing else but the sword of Gideon son of Joash, the Israelite. God has given Midian and the whole camp into his hand."
15 When Gideon heard the dream and what it meant, he bowed down to worship God. He returned to the camp of Israel and said, "Get up! The LORD has given the camp of Midian into your hand."
16 He divided the 300 men into three groups. He gave each man a ram's horn (trumpet) in one hand. In the other hand he gave a large jar with a torch inside.
17 "Watch me and do as I do," Gideon said. "When I come to the edge of the camp, do exactly as I do.
18 When I and all my men blow our horns, then you also blow your horns from all around the camp. Shout, 'For the LORD and for Gideon!'"
19 Gideon and the 100 men with him reached the edge of the camp. It was the beginning of the middle watch, just after the guards had changed. They blew their horns and broke the jars in their hands.
20 The three groups blew their horns and broke their jars. They held the torches in their left hands and the horns in their right hands. They shouted, "**A sword for the LORD and for Gideon!**"
21 Each Israelite stood in his place around the camp. The whole Midianite army ran away, crying out as they fled.

22 When the 300 rams' horns sounded, the LORD turned the men in the camp against each other with their swords. The army fled to Beth-shittah toward Zererah. They ran as far as the border of Abel-meholah near Tabbath.
23 Then the men of Israel were called out from Naphtali, Asher, and all Manasseh. They chased the Midianites.

24 Gideon sent messengers all through the hill country of Ephraim. They said, "Come down against the Midianites. Take the waters of the Jordan before them as far as Beth-barah." So all the men of Ephraim were called out. They took the waters of the Jordan as far as Beth-barah.

25 They also captured Oreb and Zeeb, the two leaders of Midian. They killed Oreb at the rock of Oreb. They killed Zeeb at the winepress of Zeeb. Then they chased the Midianites. They brought the heads of Oreb and Zeeb to Gideon on the other side of the Jordan.

title: Judges 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Then the men of Ephraim said to Gideon, "Why did you do this to us? Why did you not call us when you went to fight Midian?" They argued with him angrily.
2 But Gideon answered them, "What have I done compared to you? Are not the grapes left over by Ephraim better than the full grape harvest of Abiezer?
3 God handed Oreb and Zeeb, the two princes of Midian, over to you. What was I able to do compared to you?" After he said this, their anger calmed down.
4 Then Gideon and his three hundred men came to the Jordan and crossed it. They were very tired but still chasing the enemy.
5 So Gideon said to the men of Succoth, "Please give my soldiers some bread. They are very tired, and I am still chasing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian."
6 But the leaders of Succoth asked, "Have you already caught Zebah and Zalmunna? Why should we give bread to your army?"
7 "Very well," Gideon replied. "When the LORD hands Zebah and Zalmunna over to me, I will beat your bodies with sharp thorns from the desert!"
8 From there he went up to Penuel and asked them the same. But the men of Penuel answered the same way as the men of Succoth.
9 So Gideon told the men of Penuel, "When I come back as the winner, I will pull down this tower!"
10 Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with their army. About fifteen thousand men were left from the armies of the east. A hundred and twenty thousand soldiers with swords had already died.
11 Gideon went up by the road east of Nobah and Jogbehah. He attacked their army when they did not expect it.
12 When Zebah and Zalmunna ran away, Gideon chased them. He caught these two kings of Midian and scattered their whole army.
13 After this, Gideon son of Joash returned from the battle by the road of Heres.
14 There he caught a young man of Succoth and questioned him. The young man wrote down for him the names of the seventy-seven leaders and elders of Succoth.

15 Then Gideon went to the men of Succoth and said, "Here are Zebah and Zalmunna. You made fun of me about them. You said, 'Have you already caught Zebah and Zalmunna? Why should we give bread to your tired men?'"
16 Then he took the elders of the city. He used sharp thorns from the desert and punished the men of Succoth.
17 He also pulled down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
18 Next, Gideon asked Zebah and Zalmunna, "What kind of men did you kill at Tabor?" "Men like you," they answered. "Each one looked like the son of a king."
19 "They were my brothers," Gideon replied. "They were the sons of my mother! As the LORD truly lives, if you had let them live, I would not kill you."
20 So he said to Jether, his oldest son, "Get up and kill them." But the young man did not pull out his sword. He was afraid because he was still young.

21 Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, "Get up and kill us yourself. A man's strength matches the man." So Gideon got up and killed Zebah and Zalmunna. He took the moon-shaped decorations from the necks of their camels.
22 Then the Israelites said to Gideon, "Rule over us — you, your son, and your grandson. You have saved us from Midian."
23 But Gideon replied, "I will not rule over you. My son will not rule either. The LORD will rule over you."
24 Then he added, "Let me ask one thing of you. Each of you, give me an earring from what you took." (The enemies had gold earrings because they were Ishmaelites.)
25 "We will give them gladly," they replied. So they spread out a coat. Each man threw an earring from his plunder onto it.
26 The weight of the gold earrings was 1,700 shekels. This did not count the moon-shaped decorations, the pendants, the purple clothes of the kings of Midian, and the chains from the camels' necks.

27 From all this Gideon made an ephod (a special priestly object). He put it in Ophrah, his hometown. But soon all Israel was unfaithful to God by worshiping it there. It became a trap to Gideon and his family.
28 In this way Midian was brought low before the Israelites. They did not rise up again. So the land had rest for forty years in the days of Gideon.
29 And Jerubbaal son of Joash went home and settled down.
30 Gideon had seventy sons of his own, because he had many wives.
31 His second wife in Shechem also gave him a son. He named him Abimelech.
32 Later, Gideon son of Joash died when he was very old. He was buried in the tomb of his father Joash in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.
33 As soon as Gideon was dead, the Israelites turned away. They were unfaithful to God by worshiping the Baal gods. They set up Baal-berith as their god.
34 The Israelites did not remember the LORD their God. He had saved them from all their enemies on every side.
35 They did not show kindness to the family of Jerubbaal (that is, Gideon) for all the good things he had done for Israel.

title: Judges 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Now Abimelech son of Jerubbaal went to his mother's brothers in Shechem. He spoke to them and to his mother's whole family.
2 He said, "Please ask all the leaders of Shechem this. 'Is it better for you that seventy men rule over you, or just one man?' Remember that I am your family by blood."
3 His mother's brothers told all the leaders of Shechem these words. Their hearts wanted to follow Abimelech. They said, "He is our brother."
4 So they gave him seventy pieces of silver from the temple of Baal-berith. With this money Abimelech hired some wild and bad men to follow him.
5 He went to his father's house in Ophrah. There he killed his seventy brothers, the sons of Jerubbaal, on one big stone. But Jotham, the youngest son of Jerubbaal, lived. He hid himself.
6 Then all the leaders of Shechem and Beth-millo gathered by the oak tree at the pillar in Shechem. They made Abimelech their king.
7 Jotham heard about this. He climbed to the top of Mount Gerizim. He lifted up his voice and cried out, "Listen to me, leaders of Shechem. Then may God listen to you.
8 One day the trees went out to choose a king with oil. They said to the olive tree, 'Rule over us.'
9 But the olive tree replied, 'Should I stop giving my oil? It honors both God and man. Should I leave it to move above the trees?'
10 Then the trees said to the fig tree, 'Come and rule over us.'
11 But the fig tree replied, 'Should I stop giving my sweet and good fruit? Should I leave it to move above the trees?'
12 Then the trees said to the grapevine, 'Come and rule over us.'
13 But the grapevine replied, 'Should I stop giving my wine? It cheers both God and man. Should I leave it to move above the trees?'
14 Finally all the trees said to the thornbush, 'Come and rule over us.'
15 The thornbush replied, 'If you really make me king with oil, come and rest under my shade. But if not, may fire come out of the thornbush. May it burn up the cedar trees of Lebanon.'
16 Now did you act in truth and fairness when you made Abimelech king? Did you do well by Jerubbaal and his family? Did you do to him as is right?
17 For my father fought for you. He put his life in danger to save you from Midian.

18 But today you have risen against my father's house. You killed his seventy sons on one stone. You made Abimelech, the son of his servant woman, king over Shechem. You did this because he is your brother.
19 If you have acted in truth and fairness toward Jerubbaal and his house today, then be glad in Abimelech. May he also be glad in you.
20 But if not, may fire come from Abimelech. May it burn up the leaders of Shechem and Beth-millo. May fire also come from the leaders of Shechem and Beth-millo. May it burn up Abimelech."
21 Then Jotham ran away. He escaped to Beer and lived there. He was afraid of his brother Abimelech.
22 Abimelech ruled over Israel for three years.
23 Then God sent a spirit of hate between Abimelech and the leaders of Shechem. The leaders of Shechem acted without honesty toward Abimelech.

24 This happened so that the crime against the seventy sons of Jerubbaal would be paid back. Their blood would come back on their brother Abimelech and on the leaders of Shechem. They had helped him kill his brothers.
25 The leaders of Shechem hid men on the hilltops to attack Abimelech. They robbed everyone who passed by on the road. Someone told this to Abimelech.
26 Meanwhile, Gaal son of Ebed came with his brothers into Shechem. The leaders of Shechem trusted him.

27 They went out into the fields. They gathered grapes from their vineyards and stepped on them to make wine. They held a feast and entered the house of their god. As they ate and drank, they cursed Abimelech.

28 Then Gaal son of Ebed said, "Who is Abimelech? Who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not the son of Jerubbaal? Is not Zebul his officer? Serve the men of Hamor, the father of Shechem. Why should we serve Abimelech?
29 If only this people were under me! I would remove Abimelech. I would say to him, 'Gather your army and come out!'"
30 Zebul the governor of the city heard the words of Gaal son of Ebed. He became very angry.
31 He secretly sent men to Abimelech. He said, "Look, Gaal son of Ebed and his brothers have come to Shechem. They are turning the city against you.
32 Now then, you and your men should come tonight. Hide and wait in the fields.
33 In the morning at sunrise, get up and attack the city. When Gaal and his men come out, do to them whatever you can."
34 So Abimelech and all his troops set out by night. They hid and waited near Shechem in four groups.
35 Gaal son of Ebed went out and stood at the city gate. Just then Abimelech and his men came out from their hiding places.
36 When Gaal saw the people, he said to Zebul, "Look, people are coming down from the mountains!" But Zebul replied, "The shadows of the mountains look like men to you."
37 Then Gaal spoke again, "Look, people are coming down from the center of the land. One group is coming by way of the Diviners' Oak."
38 Zebul replied, "Where is your proud talk now? You said, 'Who is Abimelech that we should serve him?' Are these not the people you made fun of? Go out now and fight them!"
39 So Gaal went out before the leaders of Shechem. He fought against Abimelech.
40 But Abimelech chased him, and Gaal ran from him. Many men of Shechem were hurt and fell, all the way to the gate.
41 Abimelech stayed in Arumah. Zebul forced Gaal and his brothers out of Shechem.
42 The next day the people of Shechem went out into the fields. Someone told this to Abimelech.
43 So he took his men and divided them into three groups. They hid and waited in the fields. When he saw the people coming out of the city, he rose up and attacked them.
44 Abimelech and his group rushed forward. They took their place at the city gate. The other two groups rushed against everyone in the fields and killed them.
45 All that day Abimelech fought against the city. He captured it and killed its people. Then he broke down the city and spread salt over it.
46 All the leaders in the tower of Shechem heard about this. They entered the inner room of the temple of El-berith.
47 Someone told Abimelech that all the leaders in the tower of Shechem were gathered there.
48 So he and all his men went up to Mount Zalmon. Abimelech took his axe and cut a branch from the trees. He lifted it onto his shoulder. He said to his men, "Hurry! Do what you have seen me do."
49 So each man cut his own branch and followed Abimelech. They piled the branches against the inner room and set it on fire. About a thousand men and women in the tower of Shechem died there.
50 Then Abimelech went to Thebez. He camped against it and captured it.
51 But there was a strong tower inside the city. All the men, women, and leaders of the city ran there. They locked themselves in and went up to the roof of the tower.
52 Abimelech came to attack the tower. He went near its entrance to set it on fire.
53 But a woman dropped a big stone for grinding on Abimelech's head. It broke his head.

54 He quickly called the helper who carried his weapons. He said, "Pull out your sword and kill me. People must not say of me, 'A woman killed him.'" So the helper ran his sword through him, and he died.
55 When the Israelites saw that Abimelech was dead, they all went home.
56 In this way God paid back the evil that Abimelech had done to his father. Abimelech had killed his seventy brothers.
57 God also brought back all the evil of the men of Shechem on their own heads. So the curse of Jotham son of Jerubbaal came upon them.

title: Judges 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 After the time of Abimelech, a man from Issachar stood up to save Israel. His name was Tola, son of Puah, the son of Dodo. He lived in Shamir, in the hill country of Ephraim.
2 Tola led Israel as judge for twenty-three years. When he died, they buried him in Shamir.
3 After Tola, Jair the Gileadite led Israel as judge for twenty-two years.
4 He had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkeys. They had thirty towns in the land of Gilead. People still call these towns Havvoth-jair today.
5 When Jair died, they buried him in Kamon.

6 Again the Israelites did evil in the eyes of the LORD. They worshipped the Baals and the Ashtoreths. They worshipped the gods of Aram, Sidon, and Moab. They also worshipped the gods of the Ammonites and Philistines. So they turned away from the LORD and did not serve Him.
7 So the LORD became very angry with Israel. He handed them over to the Philistines and the Ammonites.
8 That same year, these enemies hurt and crushed the Israelites. They did this for eighteen years to all the Israelites on the other side of the Jordan, in Gilead, the land of the Amorites.
9 The Ammonites also crossed the Jordan to fight against Judah, Benjamin, and the house of Ephraim. Israel was in great trouble.
10 Then the Israelites called out to the LORD for help. They said, "We have done wrong against You. We have turned away from our God and worshipped the Baals."
11 The LORD replied, "When the Egyptians, Amorites, Ammonites, and Philistines hurt you,
12 and when the Sidonians, Amalekites, and Maonites crushed you, you called out to Me. Did I not save you from their hands?
13 But you have turned away from Me and worshipped other gods. So I will not save you any more.
14 Go and call out to the gods you have chosen. Let them save you when you are in trouble."
15 The Israelites said to the LORD, "We have done wrong. Do to us what You think is right. But please save us today!"
16 So they threw away the foreign gods from among them. They served the LORD. And He could not stand to see Israel suffer any more.
17 Then the Ammonites were called to fight, and they camped in Gilead. The Israelites also came together and camped at Mizpah.
18 The leaders of Gilead said to one another, "Who will start the attack against the Ammonites? He will be the leader of all the people in Gilead."

title: Judges 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Now Jephthah of Gilead was a strong and brave fighter. He was the son of a prostitute, and Gilead was his father.
2 Gilead's wife also had sons. When they grew up, they drove Jephthah away. They said, "You will get no share of our father's things. You are the son of another woman."
3 So Jephthah ran from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob. Bad men with no honor gathered around him and went with him.
4 Some time later, the Ammonites fought against Israel.
5 When they made war with Israel, the leaders of Gilead went to bring Jephthah back from the land of Tob.
6 They said to him, "Come and be our army leader. Then we can fight the Ammonites."
7 Jephthah replied to the leaders of Gilead, "Did you not hate me? Did you not push me out of my father's house? Why do you come to me now, when you are in trouble?"
8 They answered Jephthah, "That is just why we turn to you now. Come with us. Fight the Ammonites. Then you will lead all of us in Gilead."
9 But Jephthah asked them, "If you take me back to fight, and the LORD gives them to me, will I truly be your leader?"
10 The leaders of Gilead said to Jephthah, "The LORD will watch us. We will do as you say."
11 So Jephthah went with the leaders of Gilead. The people made him their leader and army chief. At Mizpah, Jephthah said all his promises before the LORD.
12 Then Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites. He asked, "What do you have against me? Why have you come to fight my land?"

13 The king of the Ammonites answered Jephthah's messengers. "When Israel came up out of Egypt, they took my land. They took it from the Arnon to the Jabbok and to the Jordan. Now give it back in peace."
14 Jephthah sent messengers again to the king of the Ammonites.
15 He told him, "This is what Jephthah says. Israel did not take the land of Moab or the Ammonites.
16 When Israel came up out of Egypt, they went through the desert. They came to the Red Sea and then to Kadesh.

17 Then Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom. They said, 'Please let us pass through your land.' But the king of Edom would not listen. They also sent to the king of Moab, but he would not agree. So Israel stayed in Kadesh.

18 Then Israel went through the desert. They went around the lands of Edom and Moab. They came to the east side of Moab and camped past the Arnon. But they did not enter Moab, because the Arnon was its edge.
19 Israel sent messengers to Sihon king of the Amorites. He ruled in Heshbon. They said, 'Please let us pass through your land to our own place.'
20 But Sihon did not trust Israel to pass through his land. So he gathered all his people. They camped at Jahaz and fought with Israel.
21 Then the LORD, the God of Israel, gave Sihon and all his people into Israel's power. Israel beat them. So Israel took all the land of the Amorites who lived there.
22 They took all the land from the Arnon to the Jabbok, and from the desert to the Jordan.
23 Now the LORD, the God of Israel, has driven the Amorites out before his people Israel. Should you now take it?
24 Do you not keep whatever your god Chemosh gives you? In the same way, we keep whatever the LORD our God has given us.
25 Are you now better than Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab? Did he ever argue or fight with Israel?
26 For three hundred years Israel has lived in Heshbon, Aroer, and their villages. We have lived in all the towns along the Arnon. Why did you not take them back in that time?
27 I have not sinned against you. But you do me wrong by making war against me. May the LORD, the Judge, decide today between Israel and the Ammonites."
28 But the king of the Ammonites did not listen to the message Jephthah sent him.
29 Then the Spirit of the LORD came on Jephthah. He passed through Gilead and Manasseh, and then through Mizpah of Gilead. From there he went on against the Ammonites.
30 Jephthah made this promise to the LORD. "If you truly give the Ammonites into my hand,
31 then whatever comes out the door of my house to meet me will belong to the LORD. When I come home safe as winner, I will offer it as a burnt offering."
32 So Jephthah crossed over to fight the Ammonites. And the LORD gave them into his hand.
33 With a great blow he destroyed twenty cities. He fought from Aroer to near Minnith, as far as Abel-keramim. So the Ammonites were beaten down before Israel.
34 When Jephthah returned home to Mizpah, his daughter came out to meet him. She had tambourines and was dancing! She was his only child. He had no other son or daughter.

35 As soon as Jephthah saw her, he tore his clothes. He said, "No! Not my daughter! You have broken my heart! You have brought great pain on me. I have given my promise to the LORD, and I cannot take it back."
36 "My father," she replied, "you have given your promise to the LORD. Do to me as you have said. For the LORD has paid back your enemies, the Ammonites, for you."
37 She also said to her father, "Let me do this one thing. Let me walk for two months in the mountains with my friends. Let me be sad that I will never marry."
38 "Go," he said. He sent her away for two months. So she left with her friends. She was sad in the mountains that she would never marry.
39 After two months, she returned to her father. He did to her as he had promised. And she had never been with a man. So it became a custom in Israel
40 that each year the young women of Israel go out for four days. They remember with sad songs the daughter of Jephthah of Gilead.

title: Judges 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Then the men of Ephraim met together and crossed the Jordan River to Zaphon. They said to Jephthah, "Why did you cross over to fight the Ammonites without us? We will burn your house down with you in it!"
2 But Jephthah replied, "My people and I had a big fight with the Ammonites. When I called you, you did not save me from them.
3 I saw that you would not help me. So I risked my life and crossed over to fight the Ammonites. And the LORD gave them into my hand. Why have you come today to fight against me?"

4 Then Jephthah gathered all the men of Gilead and fought Ephraim. The men of Gilead beat them in battle. For the men of Ephraim had said, "You Gileadites are runaways in Ephraim. You live in the lands of Ephraim and Manasseh."

5 The Gileadites took control of the river crossings of the Jordan that led to Ephraim. When a runaway from Ephraim said, "Let me cross over," the Gileadites asked him, "Are you from Ephraim?" If he said, "No,"

6 they told him, "Please say **Shibboleth**." If he said, "**Sibboleth**," because he could not say it the right way, they grabbed him and killed him at the river crossings of the Jordan. So at that time 42,000 men from Ephraim died.
7 Jephthah led Israel as judge for six years. When he died, they buried him in one of the cities of Gilead.
8 After Jephthah, Ibzan of Bethlehem led Israel as judge.

9 He had thirty sons and thirty daughters. He gave his daughters in marriage to men from outside his family. He also brought home thirty wives from other places for his sons. Ibzan led Israel as judge for seven years.
10 Then Ibzan died, and they buried him in Bethlehem.
11 After Ibzan, Elon from the tribe of Zebulun led Israel as judge for ten years.
12 Then Elon the Zebulunite died, and they buried him in Aijalon in the land of Zebulun.
13 After Elon, Abdon son of Hillel, from Pirathon, led Israel as judge.
14 He had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy donkeys. He led Israel as judge for eight years.
15 Then Abdon son of Hillel, from Pirathon, died. They buried him at Pirathon in Ephraim, in the hill country of the Amalekites.

title: Judges 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Again the Israelites did evil in the eyes of the LORD. So He handed them over to the Philistines for forty years.
2 There was a man from Zorah named Manoah, from the clan of Dan. His wife could not have children.
3 The angel of the LORD appeared to the woman. He said, "You cannot have children, but you will become pregnant and have a son.
4 Now please be careful. Do not drink wine or strong alcohol. Do not eat any unclean food.
5 Listen, you will become pregnant and have a son. No razor must ever touch his head. The boy will be a Nazirite to God from birth. He will begin to save Israel from the Philistines."
6 So the woman went and told her husband, "A man of God came to me. He looked like the angel of God, very awesome to see. I did not ask him where he came from. He did not tell me his name.
7 But he said to me, 'You will become pregnant and have a son. Do not drink wine or strong alcohol. Do not eat any unclean food. The boy will be a Nazirite to God from birth until the day he dies.'"
8 Then Manoah prayed to the LORD. "Please, O Lord, send the man of God to us again. Let him teach us how to raise the boy."
9 God listened to Manoah. The angel of God came back to the woman as she sat in the field. But her husband Manoah was not with her.
10 The woman ran quickly to tell her husband. "Look, the man who came to me the other day has come back!"
11 So Manoah got up and followed his wife. When he came to the man, he asked, "Are you the man who spoke to my wife?" "I am," he said.
12 Then Manoah asked, "When your words come true, what will be the boy's way of life and work?"
13 The angel of the LORD answered Manoah, "Your wife must do everything I told her.
14 She must not eat any food from the grape vine. She must not drink wine or strong alcohol. She must not eat any unclean food. She must do everything I have commanded her."
15 "Please stay here," Manoah said to the angel of the LORD. "We will cook a young goat for you."
16 The angel of the LORD replied, "Even if I stay, I will not eat your food. But if you prepare a burnt offering, offer it to the LORD." Manoah did not know that he was the angel of the LORD.
17 Then Manoah said to the angel of the LORD, "What is your name? When your word comes true, we want to honor you."
18 The angel of the LORD said, "Why do you ask my name? It is beyond understanding."
19 Then Manoah took a young goat and a grain offering. He offered them on a rock to the LORD. Manoah and his wife watched, and the LORD did a wonderful thing.
20 The flame went up from the altar to the sky. The angel of the LORD went up in the flame. When Manoah and his wife saw this, they fell with their faces to the ground.
21 The angel of the LORD did not appear to Manoah and his wife again. Then Manoah knew it was the angel of the LORD.
22 "We are going to die," he said to his wife. "We have seen God!"
23 But his wife replied, "If the LORD wanted to kill us, He would not have accepted our burnt offering and grain offering. He would not have shown us all these things or spoken to us this way."
24 So the woman had a son and named him Samson. The boy grew, and the LORD blessed him.
25 The Spirit of the LORD began to move him at Mahaneh-dan, between Zorah and Eshtaol.

title: Judges 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 One day Samson went down to Timnah. There he saw a young Philistine woman.
2 So he went home and told his father and mother. "I have seen a Philistine woman in Timnah. Get her for me as a wife."

3 But his father and mother said, "Can't you find a wife among our own people? Must you go to the Philistines who do not follow God?" But Samson told his father, "Get her for me. She looks good to me."
4 His father and mother did not know this came from the LORD. The LORD was looking for a reason to act against the Philistines. At that time the Philistines ruled over Israel.
5 Then Samson went down to Timnah with his father and mother. They came to the grape fields of Timnah. Suddenly a young lion came roaring at him.
6 The Spirit of the LORD came on him with great power. He tore the lion apart with his hands, as if it were a young goat. But he did not tell his father or mother what he had done.
7 Then Samson went on his way. He spoke to the woman, because she looked good to him.
8 Later Samson returned to take her as his wife. He left the road to see the dead lion. In it was a group of bees and some honey.

9 So he took some honey in his hands and ate it as he walked. When he came to his father and mother, he gave some to them, and they ate it. But he did not tell them he had taken the honey from the dead lion.
10 Then his father went to visit the woman. Samson made a big meal there, as was the custom for a bridegroom.
11 When the Philistines saw him, they chose thirty men to be with him.
12 Samson said to them, "Let me tell you a riddle. If you can solve it during the seven days of the feast, I will give you thirty fine linen clothes and thirty sets of clothes.
13 But if you cannot solve it, you must give me thirty fine linen clothes and thirty sets of clothes." They replied, "Tell us your riddle. Let us hear it."
14 So he said to them: **Out of the eater, something to eat; out of the strong, something sweet.** For three days they could not explain the riddle.

15 On the fourth day they said to Samson's wife, "Trick your husband into explaining the riddle to us. If you don't, we will burn you and your father's family to death. Did you invite us here to rob us?"

16 Then Samson's wife came to him, crying. She said, "You hate me! You don't really love me! You gave my people a riddle, but you have not explained it to me." He said, "Look, I have not explained it to my father or mother. So why should I explain it to you?"
17 She cried the whole seven days of the feast. Finally on the seventh day he told her the answer, because she begged him so much. Then she explained the riddle to her people.

18 Before sunset on the seventh day, the men of the city said to Samson: "What is sweeter than honey? And what is stronger than a lion?" So he said to them: "If you had not used my young cow to plow, you would not have solved my riddle!"

19 Then the Spirit of the LORD came on him with great power. He went down to Ashkelon and killed thirty of their men. He took their clothes and gave them to those who had solved the riddle. Very angry, Samson went back to his father's house.
20 And his wife was given to one of the men who had been with him.

title: Judges 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Later, at wheat harvest time, Samson took a young goat to visit his wife. "I want to go to her room," he said. But her father would not let him in.
2 Her father said, "I was sure you hated her, so I gave her to your friend. Is not her younger sister more beautiful? Please take her instead."
3 Samson said to them, "This time I will not be at fault when I hurt the Philistines."
4 Then Samson went out and caught three hundred foxes. He got torches and tied the foxes tail to tail. He fastened one torch between each pair of tails.

5 Then he lit the torches and let the foxes go into the grain fields of the Philistines. The fire burned the piles of grain and the grain in the fields. It burned the grape fields and olive trees too.

6 "Who did this?" the Philistines asked. They were told, "Samson did. He is the husband of the Timnite's daughter. Her father gave his wife to his friend." So the Philistines went up and burned her and her father to death.
7 Samson told them, "Because you did this, I will not rest until I pay you back."
8 He attacked them hard and killed many. Then he went down and stayed in the cave by the rock of Etam.
9 Then the Philistines went up and camped in Judah. They spread out their men near the town of Lehi.
10 The men of Judah asked, "Why have you attacked us?" The Philistines replied, "We have come to take Samson. We will pay him back for what he did to us."

11 Then three thousand men of Judah went to the cave by the rock of Etam. They asked Samson, "Do you not know the Philistines rule over us? What have you done to us?" He replied, "I did to them what they did to me."
12 They said to him, "We have come to take you and hand you over to the Philistines." Samson replied, "Swear to me you will not kill me yourselves."
13 "No," they answered, "we will not kill you. We will only tie you up well and hand you over to them." So they tied him with two new ropes and led him up from the rock.

14 When Samson arrived in Lehi, the Philistines came out shouting against him. The Spirit of the LORD came on him with great power. The ropes on his arms became like burned thread, and they fell off his hands.
15 He found the fresh jawbone of a donkey. He reached out his hand, took it, and struck down a thousand men.
16 Then Samson said, "With the jawbone of a donkey I have piled them up. With the jawbone of a donkey I have killed a thousand men."
17 When Samson finished speaking, he threw the jawbone from his hand. He named that place Ramath-lehi.
18 Samson was very thirsty. He cried out to the LORD, "You have given this great rescue through your servant. Must I now die of thirst and fall into the hands of the Philistines?"

19 So God opened up the hollow spot in Lehi, and water came out of it. When Samson drank, his strength returned, and he felt alive again. That is why he named it En-hakkore, and it is still in Lehi today.
20 Samson led Israel as judge for twenty years in the days of the Philistines.

title: Judges 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 One day, Samson went to Gaza. There he saw a bad woman and went in to spend the night with her.

2 The people of Gaza heard that Samson was there. So they surrounded the place. They hid and waited for him all night at the city gate. They stayed quiet all night and said, "We will wait until morning. Then we will kill him."

3 But Samson only stayed there until midnight. Then he got up. He took hold of the doors of the city gate and the two gate posts. He pulled them out, lock bar and all. Then he put them on his shoulders and carried them to the top of the hill near Hebron.
4 Some time later, Samson fell in love with a woman in the Valley of Sorek. Her name was Delilah.

5 The lords of the Philistines came to her. They said, "Trick him. Find out the secret of his great strength. Find out how we can beat him, tie him up, and control him. Then each of us will give you 1,100 pieces of silver."
6 So Delilah said to Samson, "Please tell me the secret of your great strength. How can someone tie you up and control you?"
7 Samson told her, "If they tie me up with seven new bow strings, still wet, I will become weak like any other man."
8 So the lords of the Philistines brought her seven new bow strings, still wet. And she tied him up with them.
9 Men were hiding in her room. She called out, "Samson, the Philistines are here!" But he broke the bow strings like thin thread burned by fire. So the secret of his strength was still unknown.
10 Then Delilah said to Samson, "You have made fun of me and lied to me! Now please tell me how someone can tie you up."
11 He replied, "If they tie me up with brand new ropes, never used, I will become weak like any other man."
12 So Delilah took new ropes and tied him up with them. Then she called out, "Samson, the Philistines are here!" Men were hiding in her room. But he broke the ropes off his arms like threads.

13 Then Delilah said to Samson, "You have made fun of me and lied to me again and again! Tell me how someone can tie you up." He told her, "If you weave the seven locks of my hair into the cloth on a loom, and you fix it tight with a pin, I will become weak like any other man."

14 So while he slept, Delilah took the seven locks of his hair. She wove them into the cloth on the loom. Then she fixed it tight with the pin. She called to him, "Samson, the Philistines are here!" But he woke up. He pulled out the pin with the loom and the cloth.
15 "How can you say, 'I love you,' when your heart is not with me?" she asked. "This is the third time you have made fun of me. You have not told me the secret of your great strength!"
16 She kept pushing him with her words every day. She begged him until he felt sick to death.

17 So Samson told her everything in his heart. He said, "**A razor has never been used on my head. I have been a Nazirite, set apart for God, from my mother's womb. If my head were shaved, my strength would leave me. Then I would become weak like any other man.**"

18 Delilah saw that he had told her everything in his heart. So she sent a message to the lords of the Philistines. She said, "Come up one more time. He has told me everything in his heart." Then the lords of the Philistines came to her. They brought the silver in their hands.
19 She rocked him to sleep on her lap. Then she called a man to shave off the seven locks of his hair. In this way she began to control him. And his strength left him.

20 Then she called out, "Samson, the Philistines are here!" Samson woke up from his sleep. He thought, "I will get free as I did before. I will shake myself loose." But he did not know that the LORD had left him.
21 Then the Philistines caught him. They pulled out his eyes. They brought him down to Gaza. There they tied him with bronze chains. They made him grind grain in the prison.
22 But the hair of his head began to grow back after it was shaved.
23 The lords of the Philistines came together to give a great offering to their god Dagon. They were full of joy and said, "Our god has given Samson our enemy into our hands."
24 When the people saw Samson, they praised their god. They said, "Our god has given our enemy into our hands. He destroyed our land and killed many of our people."
25 Their hearts were happy. So they said, "Call Samson to make us laugh." So they called Samson out of the prison to entertain them. They placed him between the pillars.
26 Samson said to the boy who held his hand, "Lead me to the pillars that hold up the temple. Let me lean on them."
27 The temple was full of men and women. All the lords of the Philistines were there. About 3,000 men and women were on the roof, watching Samson entertain them.
28 Then Samson called out to the LORD. He said, "**Sovereign LORD, remember me. Please give me strength one more time, O God. Let me pay back the Philistines with one blow for my two eyes.**"
29 Samson reached out for the two middle pillars that held up the temple. He braced himself against them. His right hand was on one pillar, and his left hand was on the other.

30 Samson said, "**Let me die with the Philistines.**" Then he pushed with all his strength. The temple fell on the lords and on all the people inside. So in his death he killed more people than he had killed in his life.
31 Then his brothers and his father's family came down. They carried him back. They buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol in the tomb of his father Manoah. He had led Israel as judge for twenty years.

title: Judges 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 There was a man named Micah from the hill country of Ephraim.
2 He said to his mother, "Someone took 1,100 pieces of silver from you. I heard you speak a curse about it. Look, I have the silver. I took it." Then his mother said, "May the LORD bless my son!"
3 He gave the 1,100 pieces of silver back to his mother. She said, "I give this silver fully to the LORD for my son. He will make a carved image and a metal idol. So I will give it back to you now."
4 So he gave the silver back to his mother. She took 200 pieces of silver and gave them to a silver worker. He made them into a carved image and a metal idol. They put them in Micah's house.
5 This man Micah had a small place to worship. He made an ephod (a special priest's vest) and some family idols. He made one of his sons his priest.
6 **In those days Israel had no king; everyone did as they saw fit**.
7 There was a young Levite (a man from the tribe of Levi) from Bethlehem in Judah. He had been living among the people of Judah.
8 This man left the city of Bethlehem in Judah. He wanted to find a new place to live. As he traveled, he came to Micah's house in the hill country of Ephraim.
9 Micah asked him, "Where are you from?" He answered, "I am a Levite from Bethlehem in Judah. I am looking for a new place to live."
10 Micah said to him, "Stay with me. Be like a father and priest to me. I will give you ten pieces of silver each year, a set of clothes, and food." So the Levite went in.
11 The Levite agreed to stay with him. The young man became like a son to Micah.
12 Micah made the Levite his priest. The young man lived in his house.
13 Then Micah said, "Now I know the LORD will bless me, because a Levite has become my priest."

title: Judges 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 In those days Israel had no king. The tribe of Dan was looking for land to settle in. Until then they had no land of their own among the tribes of Israel.

2 So the Danites sent out five strong men from Zorah and Eshtaol. They told them to look at the land and study it. The men came to the hill country of Ephraim. They stopped at Micah's house and spent the night there.
3 While they were near Micah's house, they knew the voice of the young Levite. They went over and asked him, "Who brought you here? What are you doing in this place? Why are you here?"
4 He answered, "Micah has done this and that for me. He has hired me to be his priest."
5 Then they said to him, "Please ask God for us. We want to know if our trip will be safe and good."
6 The priest told them, "Go in peace. The LORD is watching over your trip."

7 So the five men left and came to Laish. They saw that the people were living safely, like the Sidonians. The people were quiet and not worried. They had everything they needed. They had no harsh ruler. They were far from the Sidonians and had no friends to help them.
8 When the men returned to Zorah and Eshtaol, their brothers asked, "What did you find?"
9 They answered, "Come, let us go up and attack them! We have seen the land, and it is very good. Why do you wait? Do not be slow to go and take the land!
10 When you enter, you will find a people who are not worried. The land is wide open. God has given it into your hand. Nothing on earth is missing there."
11 So six hundred men from the tribe of Dan left Zorah and Eshtaol. They were ready with weapons for war.
12 They went up and camped at Kiriath-jearim in Judah. That is why the place west of Kiriath-jearim is called Mahaneh-dan to this day.
13 From there they traveled to the hill country of Ephraim. They came to Micah's house.
14 The five men who had explored the land of Laish said to their brothers, "Did you know one of these houses has an ephod, family idols, a carved image, and a metal idol? Now think about what to do."
15 So they turned aside and came to the home of the young Levite at Micah's house. They greeted him.
16 The six hundred Danites stood at the gate, ready with their weapons for war.
17 The five men who had explored the land went inside. They took the carved image, the ephod, the family idols, and the metal idol. The priest stood at the gate with the six hundred armed men.
18 When they took these things from Micah's house, the priest asked them, "What are you doing?"
19 They told him, "Be silent. Come with us. Be like a father and priest to us. Is it better to be a priest for one man's house, or for a tribe and family in Israel?"
20 So the priest was glad. He took the ephod, the family idols, and the carved image. He went with the people.
21 They put their small children, their animals, and their things in front of them. Then they turned and went on their way.
22 They were some distance from Micah's house. Then the men near Micah's house gathered and caught up with the Danites.
23 They called out after them. The Danites turned to face them. They said to Micah, "What is wrong with you? Why have you called out so many men?"
24 He replied, "You took the gods I made. You took my priest. You went away. What else do I have? How can you say to me, 'What is wrong with you?'"
25 The Danites said to him, "Do not shout at us. Angry men might attack you. Then you and your family will die."
26 So the Danites went on their way. Micah turned and went back home. He saw they were too strong for him.
27 They took the idols Micah had made, and his priest. They went to Laish, to a quiet people who were not worried. They killed them with their swords and burned the city.
28 No one came to save the people. The city was far from Sidon and had no friends to help them. It lay in a valley near Beth-rehob. The Danites rebuilt the city and lived there.
29 They named it Dan, after their forefather Dan, the son of Israel. The city was called Laish before.
30 The Danites put up idols for themselves. Jonathan son of Gershom, the son of Moses, and his sons were priests for the tribe of Dan. They served until the day the land was taken away.
31 They set up Micah's carved image for themselves. It stayed there the whole time God's house was in Shiloh.

title: Judges 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 In those days, Israel had no king. A Levite lived far away in the hill country of Ephraim. He took a second wife from Bethlehem in Judah.
2 But she left him in anger. She went back to her father's house in Bethlehem in Judah. She stayed there four months.

3 Then her husband went after her to speak kindly and bring her home. He took his servant and two donkeys. The girl brought him into her father's house. Her father was glad to see him and welcomed him.
4 Her father asked him to stay. So the Levite stayed three days. They ate, drank, and slept there.
5 On the fourth day, they got up early to leave. But the girl's father said to him, "Eat some bread first to feel strong. Then you can go."
6 So the two men sat down and ate and drank together. Then the girl's father said, "Please stay one more night and enjoy yourself."
7 The man got up to leave, but his father-in-law urged him to stay. So he stayed that night too.
8 On the fifth day, he got up early to leave. But the girl's father said, "Please eat first." So they waited until late afternoon, and the two of them ate.

9 Then the man got up to leave with his second wife and his servant. His father-in-law said to him, "Look, the day is almost over. Please stay the night. Enjoy yourself here. Then leave early tomorrow for home."
10 But the man would not stay another night. He got up and left. He came near Jebus (that is, Jerusalem). He had his two donkeys and his second wife.
11 When they were near Jebus, the day was almost gone. The servant said to his master, "Please, let us stop in this city of Jebus and stay the night."
12 But his master said, "We will not stop in a foreign city. No Israelites live there. We will go on to Gibeah."
13 He said, "Come, let us try to reach Gibeah or Ramah. We can stay the night there."
14 So they kept walking. The sun went down as they came near Gibeah in Benjamin.
15 They stopped to stay the night in Gibeah. The Levite went in and sat down in the city square. But no one took them home for the night.
16 That evening an old man came in from his work in the field. He was from the hill country of Ephraim. He now lived in Gibeah. (The men of that town were Benjamites.)
17 He looked up and saw the traveler in the city square. The old man asked, "Where are you going? Where have you come from?"

18 The Levite said, "We are coming from Bethlehem in Judah. We are going to the far hill country of Ephraim, where I am from. I went to Bethlehem in Judah. Now I am going to the house of the LORD. But no one has taken me home.
19 We have straw and food for our donkeys. We have bread and wine for me, the woman, and the young man with me. We have all we need."
20 "Peace to you," said the old man. "I will give you all you need. Only do not stay the night in the square."
21 So he brought him to his house and fed his donkeys. They washed their feet and ate and drank.

22 While they were enjoying themselves, bad men of the city came around the house. They beat on the door. They said to the old man who owned the house, "Bring out the man who came to your house, so we can abuse him!"
23 The owner of the house went out to them. He said, "No, my brothers, do not do this evil thing! This man is a guest in my house. Do not do this terrible thing.
24 Look, let me bring out my young daughter and the man's second wife. Do what you want with them. But do not do such an evil thing to this man."
25 But the men would not listen to him. So the Levite took his second wife and sent her outside to them. The men attacked her all night. At dawn they let her go.
26 Early that morning, the woman went back to the house where her master was staying. She fell down at the door and lay there until it was light.
27 In the morning, her master got up. He opened the doors of the house to go on his way. There was his second wife, fallen at the door. Her hands were on the doorstep.
28 "Stand up," he said to her. "Let us go." But she did not answer. So the man put her on his donkey and set out for home.
29 When he reached his house, he took a knife. He took hold of his second wife and cut her body into twelve pieces. Then he sent the pieces through all the land of Israel.
30 Everyone who saw it said, "Nothing like this has happened since Israel came out of Egypt. Think about it. Talk together. Say what to do!"

title: Judges 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 Then all the Israelites came out — from Dan to Beersheba, and from Gilead. They met as one before the LORD at Mizpah.
2 The leaders of all the tribes of Israel stood before God's people. There were 400,000 foot soldiers with swords.
3 The Benjamites heard that Israel had gone up to Mizpah. The Israelites asked, "Tell us, how did this evil thing happen?"
4 The Levite, the husband of the dead woman, answered. "I and my second wife came to Gibeah in Benjamin to spend the night.
5 During the night, the men of Gibeah came after me. They surrounded the house and wanted to kill me. They hurt my second wife, and she died.
6 Then I took her body, cut it into pieces, and sent the pieces through Israel. They had done an evil and shameful act in Israel.
7 Look, all you Israelites — give your advice and decision here and now."
8 Then all the people stood as one and said, "None of us will go home to his tent or house.
9 This is what we will do to Gibeah. We will go against it as the lot decides.

10 We will take ten men out of every hundred from all the tribes of Israel. We will take a hundred out of every thousand, and a thousand out of every ten thousand. They will bring food for the army when they punish Gibeah in Benjamin for this terrible act."
11 So all the men of Israel gathered as one, united against the city.
12 The tribes of Israel sent men through the tribe of Benjamin. They said, "What is this evil thing that has happened among you?
13 Hand over the wicked men of Gibeah. We will put them to death and remove this evil from Israel." But the Benjamites would not listen to their fellow Israelites.
14 From their cities, the Benjamites came together at Gibeah to fight Israel.
15 That day, the Benjamites gathered 26,000 men with swords from their cities. There were also 700 chosen men from Gibeah.
16 Among all these soldiers were 700 chosen left-handers. Each one could sling a stone at a hair and not miss.
