17 Israel, not counting Benjamin, gathered 400,000 men with swords. Each one was an experienced fighter.
18 The Israelites went up to Bethel and asked God, "Who of us will go up first to fight Benjamin?" The LORD answered, "Judah will be first."
19 The next morning, the Israelites set out and camped near Gibeah.
20 The men of Israel went out to fight Benjamin. They got ready for battle at Gibeah.
21 The Benjamites came out of Gibeah. They cut down 22,000 Israelites in the field that day.
22 But the army of Israel took courage. They got ready again in the same place as the first day.
23 They went up and cried before the LORD until evening. They asked Him, "Should we go to battle again against our brothers the Benjamites?" The LORD said, "Go up against them."
24 On the second day, the Israelites went forward against the Benjamites.
25 That same day, the Benjamites came out from Gibeah. They cut down another 18,000 Israelites, all with swords.
26 Then all the Israelites went up to Bethel. They sat and cried before the LORD. They fasted that day until evening. They brought burnt offerings and peace offerings to the LORD.
27 The Israelites asked the LORD for help. In those days, the ark of God's covenant was there.

28 Phinehas son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron, served before it. The Israelites asked, "Should we go out to battle again against our brothers, or should we stop?" The LORD answered, "Fight, for tomorrow I will give them into your hand."
29 So Israel set a hidden trap around Gibeah.
30 On the third day, the Israelites went up against the Benjamites. They got ready against Gibeah as before.

31 The Benjamites came out against them and were drawn away from the city. They began to attack as before. They killed about thirty men of Israel in the fields and on the roads. One road led up to Bethel and the other to Gibeah.
32 "We are beating them as before," said the Benjamites. But the Israelites said, "Let us run back and draw them away from the city."
33 So all the men of Israel left their places and got ready at Baal-tamar. The Israelites in hiding rushed out from west of Gibeah.
34 Then 10,000 chosen men from all Israel made a strong attack against Gibeah. The battle was hard. But the Benjamites did not see that trouble was upon them.
35 The LORD defeated Benjamin in front of Israel. That day, the Israelites killed 25,100 Benjamites, all with swords.
36 Then the Benjamites saw that they had lost. Now the men of Israel had run back before Benjamin. They trusted the hidden trap they had set against Gibeah.
37 The men in hiding rushed against Gibeah. They moved in and struck the whole city with the sword.
38 The men of Israel had agreed on a signal with the men in hiding. When they sent up a big cloud of smoke from the city,
39 the men of Israel would turn in the battle. The Benjamites had begun to strike them down, killing about thirty men of Israel. They said, "They are beaten before us as in the first battle."
40 But when the cloud of smoke began to rise from the city, the Benjamites looked back. They saw the whole city going up in smoke.
41 Then the men of Israel turned back on them. The men of Benjamin were afraid when they saw that trouble had come upon them.
42 So they ran from the men of Israel toward the wilderness. But the battle caught up with them. The men coming out of the cities struck them down there.
43 They surrounded the Benjamites and chased them. They easily caught them near Gibeah on the east.
44 And 18,000 Benjamites fell, all strong fighters.
45 Then the Benjamites turned and ran toward the wilderness to the rock of Rimmon. Israel cut down 5,000 men on the roads. They caught more at Gidom and struck down 2,000 more.
46 That day, 25,000 Benjamite men with swords fell, all strong fighters.
47 But 600 men turned and ran into the wilderness to the rock of Rimmon. They stayed there four months.

48 The men of Israel turned back against the rest of Benjamin. They struck all the cities with the sword, including the animals and everything else they found. They burned down all the cities along the way.

title: Judges 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Judges
paragraphs: 1 The men of Israel had made a strong promise at Mizpah. They said, "None of us will give his daughter to a Benjamite."
2 So the people came to Bethel and sat before God until evening. They cried out loud with deep sadness.
3 They cried, "O LORD, God of Israel, why has this happened? Today one tribe of Israel is missing!"
4 Early the next day, the people got up. They built an altar there. They gave burnt offerings and peace offerings.
5 The Israelites asked, "Who among all the tribes of Israel did not come to the meeting before the LORD?" They had made a strong promise. Anyone who did not come to Mizpah would be killed.
6 The Israelites felt sad for their brothers, the Benjamites. They said, "Today one tribe is gone from Israel.
7 What should we do for the men who are left? We have promised the LORD not to give them our daughters."
8 So they asked, "Which tribe of Israel did not come to the LORD at Mizpah?" No one from Jabesh-gilead had come to the camp for the meeting.
9 When the people were counted, no one from Jabesh-gilead was there.
10 So the group sent 12,000 brave men. They told them, "Go and kill the people of Jabesh-gilead with the sword. Kill women and children too.
11 This is what you must do. Destroy completely every male. Destroy every woman who has been with a man."
12 They found four hundred young women in Jabesh-gilead. These women had never been with a man. They brought them to the camp at Shiloh in the land of Canaan.
13 Then the whole group sent a message of peace to the Benjamites at the rock of Rimmon.
14 So the Benjamites came back. They were given the women left alive from Jabesh-gilead. But there were not enough women for them all.
15 The people felt sad for Benjamin. The LORD had left a gap in the tribes of Israel.
16 Then the leaders of the group said, "What can we do for the men left? The women of Benjamin are gone.
17 The men who are left in Benjamin need children. A tribe of Israel must not be gone forever.
18 But we cannot give them our daughters." The Israelites had promised, "A curse on the man who gives a wife to a Benjamite."
19 Then they said, "Look, there is a feast every year to the LORD in Shiloh. Shiloh is north of Bethel, east of the road from Bethel to Shechem, and south of Lebonah."
20 So they told the Benjamites, "Go and hide in the vineyards.

21 Watch carefully. The daughters of Shiloh will come out to dance. Then each of you must come out of the vineyards. Catch a wife for yourself from the daughters of Shiloh. Then go back to the land of Benjamin.

22 When their fathers or brothers complain to us, we will say this. 'Please help them, because we did not get wives for them in the war. You did not actually give them your daughters, so you have no guilt.'"
23 The Benjamites did as they were told. They took as many women as they needed from the dancers they caught. They went back to their own land. They rebuilt their cities and lived in them.
24 At that time, each Israelite went back to his own tribe and family. Each one went to his own land.
25 **In those days Israel had no king; everyone did as they saw fit**.
